# Cognitive Overlay Design — Final Master Archive v12

Generated: **2026-04-21**

This archive consolidates the full project into a Markdown-first system: definitions, method names, proof logic, research synthesis, experiment protocols, production workflows, adaptive-engine specs, and legacy artifacts.

## Core field hierarchy

| Level | Name | Code | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| Field | Cognitive Overlay Design | COD | The design of text, timing, motion, placement, and emphasis in video to improve comprehension, retention, accessibility, and attention efficiency. |
| Short-form branch | Physiological Retention Typography | PRT | Short-form/mobile overlay design based on vision, gaze, working memory, saliency, fatigue, and scroll behavior. |
| Method family | Semantic Compression Captions | SCCF | Methods that reduce speech into compact meaning units instead of verbatim subtitles. |
| Named method | Bionic Compression Captioning | BCC | Semantic compression plus operator anchors and bionic concept anchors. |
| Advanced protocol | Bionic Compression Captioning Plus | BCC+ | BCC plus gaze-safe placement, visual-load adaptation, muted-first design, and dual-layer captioning. |
| Future product | Adaptive Semantic Overlay Engine | ASOE | A system that routes overlay methods by context and evidence. |

## Operating law

> Do not make viewers read faster. Engineer the video so they need fewer fixations to understand more.
